text:
King's College Hospital 

Department of Neurosciences 

Clinic Date: 05 April 2023 

Dr A. Patel
Consultant Neurologist  
King's College Hospital  
Department of Neurosciences, King's College Hospital, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr A. Patel
Consultant Neurologist,  

Daniel Harris, DOB: 14-03-1982, Hospital No: KCH742915 NHS No. 9065183274  
78 Brookfield Road, London, SE15 2LP

Thank you for asking me to review your patient. He attended with his partner and provided a clear diary of recent events. Unfortunately, there is evidence of clinical deterioration over the past month. He reports increased day-to-day difficulty with concentration, intermittent word-finding pauses, and escalating fatigue and irritability that he attributes to his current polytherapy. Adverse effects are becoming bothersome (notably daytime somnolence, unsteadiness on rapid head turns, and blunted mood), yet seizure control remains suboptimal. Over the last week he recorded five focal cognitive and six focal non-motors in last week, occurring at variable times without an obvious precipitant. Several of these were clustered over two consecutive mornings, with prolonged post-event disorientation on one occasion. His partner also notes brief nocturnal arousals with confusion, though no clear witnessed generalised convulsions.

He remains adherent to medication and has not missed doses; he denies alcohol or illicit substance use. Sleep has been fragmented, in part due to early-morning awakenings. He is using a smartphone seizure diary, and we reviewed consistent timestamped entries. There has been no recent intercurrent illness. He has not driven and understands the DVLA guidance as previously discussed.

Examination today was non-focal. Gait was steady on slow turns but he felt light-headed on tandem stepping. No papilloedema. Cardiovascular and systemic review unremarkable. Mood was subdued but there was no thought disorder or psychotic features. 

Investigations to date include a prior routine EEG (last year) that was non-diagnostic and a brain MRI (18 months ago) reported as structurally unremarkable. Given the current pattern and the worsening frequency and impact, I am concerned that, despite polytherapy, control is not adequate and tolerability is poor. We discussed the need to clarify the electroclinical pattern and to consider rationalisation of treatment if safe to do so. He is agreeable to expedited prolonged video-EEG monitoring as an inpatient, and I will request this urgently. I will also arrange updated bloods (FBC, U&E, LFT, bone profile, vitamin D, thyroid function) and drug levels where available to check for toxicity or interactions contributing to the adverse effects.

We reviewed general measures: regular sleep schedule, hydration, and avoidance of missed meals. He and his partner are aware of seizure first aid and have a plan to escalate if events become longer or different in character. I provided contact details for our epilepsy nurse specialist for interim support while we await telemetry. 

I will update you once monitoring is completed and results are available. In the meantime, I have not altered his current regimen today due to the risk of further destabilisation, but the trajectory is concerning, and I anticipate we may need to rationalise therapy soon depending on monitoring findings and tolerability.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Over the last week he recorded five focal cognitive and six focal non-motors in last week
five focal cognitive and six focal non-motors in last week

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit seizure counts and timeframe. The letter states: "Over the last week he recorded five focal cognitive and six focal non-motors in last week". This provides a clear total over a defined period. Sum the seizures: 5 + 6 = 11 over 1 week. No other competing frequency provided. Therefore, normalize to '11 per week'.
seizure_frequency_number: Over the last week he recorded five focal cognitive and six focal non-motors in last week
reference: five focal cognitive and six focal non-motors in last week
